data.title: Falcon Finance Introduces $50 Million Fund for Real-World Asset Development
data.description: Falcon Finance has unveiled a $50 million ecosystem fund to bolster infrastructure development associated with real-world assets, including tokenized U.S. Treasurys and precious metals. According to NS3.AI, the fund will focus on investments in government bonds, fixed-income products, and tokenized real-world asset protocols, as well as infrastructure related to gold, silver, and platinum. The fund is divided equally, with half dedicated to capital investments and the other half allocated to FF token incentives, which come with vesting schedules.

data.title: Canadian Fugitive Accused of $65 Million Crypto Theft
data.description:
A 21-year-old Canadian fugitive, Andean Medjedovic, is accused of stealing $65 million in assets from Indexed Finance and KyberSwap through hacking. According to Foresight News, Medjedovic was arrested in Belgrade, Serbia, in August 2024, but was released after a Serbian court denied the Netherlands' extradition request. He is believed to be hiding in Bosnia.

Investigations reveal that Medjedovic used his skills in mathematics and programming to attack Indexed Finance at the age of 18, profiting $16.5 million. He later targeted KyberSwap in 2023, gaining $48 million. Medjedovic allegedly used a fake Slovak passport and traveled across countries including Brazil, Dubai, and Spain. The U.S. Department of Justice accuses him of laundering money through mixers like Tornado Cash.

Despite facing arrest warrants from multiple countries, Medjedovic has denied all charges in previous court statements, claiming he continues to earn $100,000 monthly as a freelance cryptocurrency professional.

data.title: Crypto Markets Brace as Trump’s Kevin Warsh Fed Pick Puts Independence at Risk – Here Is Why
data.description:
"Crypto markets are preparing for potential volatility and uncertainty in response to the recent announcement regarding Trump’s nomination of Kevin Warsh as a candidate for a position at the Federal Reserve. This development raises concerns about the independence of the Federal Reserve, as Warsh's past comments and ideas suggest he may not align with traditional central bank principles. Here, we will explore the implications of this appointment, the historical context surrounding the Fed's independence, and how these factors could impact the crypto markets moving forward."
source: https://blocknews.com/crypto-markets-brace-as-trumps-kevin-warsh-fed-pick-puts-independence-at-risk-here-is-why/

data.title: Goodbye Powell – President Trump officially selects Kevin Warsh as the new Federal Reserve Chair
data.description:
President Trump nominates Kevin Warsh to replace Jerome Powell as Federal Reserve chair, triggering sharp moves in the dollar, bonds, gold, and Bitcoin.
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/trump-selects-kevin-warsh-federal-chair/

data.title: Democratic senators are calling for a pause in Walsh's nomination process until the Justice Department concludes its investigation into current Chairman Powell.
data.description: Massachusetts Democratic Senator Elizabeth Warren expressed several concerns on Friday regarding Trump's decision to nominate Warsh. As the ranking Democrat on the Senate Banking Committee (and the first hurdle in Warsh's confirmation process), Warren questioned whether Warsh could lead the Federal Reserve without White House influence. "Trump has stated that anyone who disagrees with him cannot become Fed Chair," Warren said in a statement. "Former Fed Governor Kevin Warsh—who was more concerned with helping Wall Street than with millions of unemployed Americans after the 2008 financial crisis—has clearly passed the loyalty test." Warren called on Republicans to hold off on Warsh's nomination until the Justice Department concludes its investigation into current Chairman Powell. "Any Republican who claims to care about the Fed's independence should refuse to proceed with this nomination until Trump stops his 'political witch hunt' against current Fed Chair and Governor Lisa Cook," she stated. (Jinshi)

data.title: Copper Demand Expected to Surge Due to AI and Electrification
data.description: Copper demand is projected to increase significantly through 2040, primarily driven by advancements in AI infrastructure, electrification, and a decreasing supply that is expected to peak by 2030. According to NS3.AI, tokenized copper assets on blockchain platforms are beginning to show growth, suggesting early interest from the crypto market. Industry experts predict that 2026 will be a pivotal year for tokenization in the metals sector, presenting new opportunities for investment and trading.

data.title: Ripple Launches Treasury Platform Following $1 Billion GTreasury Deal
data.description: Ripple, associated with XRP, has unveiled a new treasury platform following a $1 billion agreement with GTreasury. According to NS3.AI, the platform allows businesses to manage cash, stablecoins, and tokenized assets within a single system. This development notably decreases cross-border settlement durations from days to mere seconds.

data.title: Bitget has launched USDT-based XPD perpetual contracts and simultaneously launched MEGA pre-market contracts.
data.description: According to an official announcement, Bitget has launched USDT-based XPT and XPD perpetual contracts, with leverage ranging from 1 to 100x. Contract trading bots will also be available simultaneously. In addition, Bitget has launched MEGA pre-market contracts with leverage ranging from 1 to 5x. Users can trade through the official website or the Bitget app.

data.title: Republican Senator Tillis Maintains Opposition to Federal Reserve Nominee Confirmation
data.description: U.S. Republican Senator Thom Tillis has expressed his continued opposition to the confirmation of any nominee for the Federal Reserve Chair, despite acknowledging the qualifications of the current nominee, Philip Walsh. According to Jin10, Tillis stated that his stance remains unchanged and he will oppose the confirmation process for any candidate put forward for the position. This announcement comes amid ongoing discussions regarding the leadership of the Federal Reserve and its impact on economic policy.

data.title: NASA Announces Artemis II Launch Schedule
data.description: NASA has revealed the schedule for the Artemis II mission, including the wet dress rehearsal and launch window. According to Jin10, the launch is planned for no earlier than February 8. This mission marks a significant step in NASA's efforts to return humans to the Moon, following the success of the Artemis I mission. The wet dress rehearsal is a critical pre-launch test that ensures all systems are functioning correctly before the actual launch. NASA's Artemis program aims to establish a sustainable human presence on the Moon by the end of the decade, paving the way for future exploration of Mars.

data.title: XRP loses $7 billion from its market cap in mega crash
data.description:
While the entire cryptocurrency market suffered a bloodbath in the 24 hours between the morning of January 29 and January 30, none of the major digital assets experienced a larger relative drop than XRP. Specifically, the popular token is down 7% in the 24 hours – a move that led to a total weekly downturn of 8.41% – and is, at press time on Friday, changing hands at $1.75
source: https://finbold.com/xrp-loses-7-billion-from-its-market-cap-in-mega-crash/

data.title: US Democratic Senator: No Republican should agree to Warsh's nomination until Trump halts the Federal Reserve investigation.
data.description: U.S. Democratic Senator Elizabeth Warren stated that no Republican should agree to advance Warsh's nomination until President Trump halts the Federal Reserve investigation. (Jinshi)

data.title: Senator Warren Urges Halt on Fed Investigation Before Advancing Nomination
data.description: U.S. Senator Elizabeth Warren has called for a pause on the Federal Reserve investigation before any Republican agrees to advance the nomination of Judy Shelton. According to BlockBeats, Warren emphasized that U.S. President Donald Trump should not halt the investigation into the Federal Reserve. This statement comes amid discussions surrounding Shelton's nomination to the Federal Reserve Board. Warren's remarks highlight the ongoing political tensions regarding the Federal Reserve's operations and leadership appointments.

data.title: Bitcoin Faces Critical Turning Point Amid Investor Behavior Shift
data.description:
On January 30, CryptoQuant analyst MorenoDV provided insights into Bitcoin's future market trends based on the Adjusted Spent Output Profit Ratio (aSOPR) indicator. According to BlockBeats, despite Bitcoin's price reaching new highs, climbing from approximately $40,000 to over $100,000 since early 2024, the aSOPR indicator presents a contrasting picture. It reveals a pattern of lower highs and lower lows, indicating key information about investor behavior. Each time Bitcoin hits a new price peak, holders are increasingly quick to take profits, suggesting a gradual weakening of confidence with each rally.

MorenoDV noted that the market is currently at a critical juncture. The aSOPR consistently respects a descending channel, with touches on the upper boundary corresponding to local price tops and touches on the lower boundary indicating local bottoms. Presently, Bitcoin is testing the lower boundary of this descending channel amid extreme fear, with approximately one-third of the total supply in a state of unrealized loss. Historically, such conditions (aSOPR below 1.0 combined with significant unrealized losses) have often presented tactical buying opportunities before short-term rebounds. However, the market now stands at a crossroads. If the current support level fails and other technical indicators confirm bearish momentum, the market may enter a phase of capitulation.

data.title: Pressure Mounts on Walsh at Federal Reserve Amid Treasury Selection Process
data.description: Alan Rappeport, a journalist from The New York Times, reports that the selection process led by Treasury Secretary Besant is putting significant pressure on Walsh at the Federal Reserve. According to Jin10, during U.S. President Donald Trump's first term, Treasury Secretary Mnuchin gradually lost Trump's trust after Powell failed to meet Trump's expectations. Although Trump currently appreciates Besant's performance at the Treasury, there is a possibility that Besant could face a similar fate as Mnuchin if Walsh does not deliver the expected rate cuts.
